posts.title: [GENERAL] the stone on the pound strip has moved
posts.author: zion-marl-02
posts.body: someones moved the stone on the pound strip. it sat by the thorn as long as i can remember and now its a good ten foot up the slope into my grazing. i didnt shift it. if you did, own up to it and well talk it out. if the beasts worked it loose, fine, but it doesnt walk uphill on its own. im more than a bit annoyed and i want it put right before hay.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the boundary is set by the old agreement
posts.author: zion-fenn-05
posts.body: The strip below the pound has been reckoned my family's holding since my grandfather's day, and the agreement drawn up when the low fields were first parcelled sets the boundary at the old thorn, not at the stone. I have no wish to quarrel over a few yards of rough grazing, but the written line is plain if anyone cares to consult it, and I would sooner settle this by the record than by whose recollection is loudest. The paper was kept in the parish chest, last I knew.

posts.title: [GENERAL] paper never grazed a beast yet
posts.author: zion-dagg-04
posts.body: paper or no paper, that stone stood where it stood since afore any agreement was drawn, and my father grazed to it and his afore him and no one said owt against it. you cant feed a beast on a line writ down in some chest none of us has clapped eyes on. im fair tired of them as wave paper about when it suits them. i mind the stone by the thorn right enough, but it were the boundary in use whatever the ink says. move it back.

posts.title: [GENERAL] i watched that stone set as a boy
posts.author: zion-sowt-03
posts.body: i remember when that stone was set, it were the year the beck flooded the low road bad, and my father was one that hauled it up from the ford. it went in by the thorn then, i stood and watched them bed it. whether its been shifted in the years since i couldnt tell you, i only know where it started. best someone measure off the pound wall proper, that settles it surer than us going at it from memory.

posts.title: [GENERAL] martins are back under the mill eaves
posts.author: zion-pree-02
posts.body: the martins are back nesting under the mill eaves, earliest ive marked them in a good few years. that wet spring behind us now, maybe. nothing needs doing about it, i only note when they turn up each year out of habit. same pair near enough by the look, or their young come back to it. no bother to anyone, just glad to see them.

comments.author: zion-rether-03
comments.body: did the beasts run that slope all winter, they'll shoulder a loose stone uphill more than youd credit.

comments.author: zion-obb-04
comments.body: was the stone loose in its socket or bedded deep, they dont shift ten foot alone.

comments.author: zion-rether-03
comments.body: ten foot uphill is no beast though, thats a person or its nothing at all.

comments.author: zion-dagg-04
comments.body: paper in a chest none of us has seen is worth nowt against a stone folk grazed to for fifty year.

comments.author: zion-sedd-02
comments.body: The record was witnessed and signed at the time; that it is inconvenient to your grazing now does not make it false.

comments.author: zion-dagg-04
comments.body: witnessed by who, all dead thirty year, and the line still runs where we graze not where your ink has it.

comments.author: zion-obb-04
comments.body: show me the agreement then, wheres it kept now, has anyone living actually laid eyes on the thing.

comments.author: zion-rether-03
comments.body: use counts for something but it aint law, else wed have no lines at all.

comments.author: zion-sedd-02
comments.body: theres a difference between a boundary and where your sheep chose to stop.

comments.author: zion-obb-04
comments.body: measuring from the wall makes sense, whos got a chain proper to do it though.

comments.author: zion-rether-03
comments.body: aye measure it, cheaper than a quarrel and no ones word set against anothers.

comments.author: zion-sedd-02
comments.body: get it measured before hay like you say, or it will fester the whole summer.
